name: dbt_corpus_convergence
host_notes: Windows / op run + uv run. Strategy mix: KeywordSearch + SemanticSearch + SeededGraphSearch (returns empty when no seed_ids). Seed extraction via word-boundary regex against entity short-name index built at corpus load.

providers.chat: unused (no LLM in B-1 setup or round loop — manifest descriptions are rich enough)

comparison_to_baseline.biggest_skill_delta: downstream_lineage flipped from 0% (no GraphSearch) to 100% (seeded GraphSearch). 12-round smoke and 100-round full both show this clearly.

interpretation.useful_delta_positive: First positive useful_delta on a real-shaped corpus. The dual-loop is doing work: 6 advisories generated (sample size gates met), 54 boosts applied to useful items, 258 noise tags. Useful-fraction climbs from 0.235 (first quarter) to 0.382 (last quarter) — concrete evidence the system improves with use on a corpus authors didn't shape to make the loop succeed.
interpretation.weighted_delta_slightly_positive: +0.018 — small but POSITIVE. Phase A's was -0.110 because LLM-rich descriptions kept being trimmed by noise loops. On dbt the descriptions are fewer + sparser, so the noise-trim hurts breadth less.
interpretation.advisory_boost_count_54: fitness loop boosted 54 advisories — these are items the loop identified as reinforcing-the-right-answer based on observed feedback. Phase A had 0 boosted because nothing was failing.
interpretation.skills_at_0_percent: multi_hop_lineage / layer_classification / test_coverage / test_cross_reference. Three distinct root causes: (a) max_items=8 budget cuts items when GraphSearch and keyword/semantic don't dedup (different item_id forms — 'doc:X' vs 'X'); (b) some queries have no extractable entity name in the intent (layer_classification, test_coverage); (c) GraphSearch depth=2 default may miss the source layer for full upstream lineage. All three are addressable in follow-ups.

decision_recorded: Phase B-1 with seeded GraphSearch produces the first credible convergence chart on a real-shaped corpus. Cost is essentially free ($0.000043 for 100 rounds). The 4 zero-scoring skills are sitting there as obvious follow-up wins: dedup PackItems by canonical entity_id (item_id collapsing across keyword/semantic/graph), expand seed extraction to non-name terms, raise GraphSearch depth for explicit lineage queries. Phase B-1 acceptance criteria per plan §8: convergence.useful_delta ≥ 0 ✓ (+0.146); loops.advisories_suppressed_total ≥ 1 ✗ (0 — corpus is clean enough that no advisory hits the suppression threshold); GraphSearch A/B shows non-zero contribution ✓ (downstream_lineage flipped 0%→100%). Two of three pass; suppression-evidence remains a future signal that may require a regime-shift demo on this corpus or a noisier query distribution.
follow_ups_pending: PackItem dedup by canonical entity_id (collapse 'doc:X' vs 'X' across strategies) — would unblock multi_hop_lineage and test_* skills which currently waste pack budget on cross-strategy duplicates
Seed extraction beyond entity names — phrase-based ('mart-layer', 'staging') or LLM-based intent classification — to unblock layer_classification skill
Higher GraphSearch depth (3+) for explicit upstream-lineage queries — covers the source layer that 'full upstream of customers mart' needs
100-round no-GraphSearch baseline run for clean before/after chart — currently only the 12-round smoke exists as the no-GraphSearch reference
LLM-generated test descriptions to replace the templated ones — would help test_* skills without solving the dedup issue
